commentaries.author: Ephrem The Syrian
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh “So he set out from there and found Elisha son of Shaphat. He passed by him and threw his mantle over him. ” With his mantle Elijah took Elisha from farming to prophesying. From the symbolic point of view, Elisha represents the type of the apostles to whom our Lord said in the Gospel, “So stay here in the city of Jerusalem until you have been clothed with power from high. ” Therefore the mantle of Elijah signified the gifts of the Spirit which the apostles would receive.

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Kiss, and bid them adieu. (Menochius)
To thee. I have no farther orders. Obey the Spirit of God. Hoc age. Hebrew, "for what have I done to thee? "Did I require thee to follow me? Act as God may direct thee. Yet remember the ceremony which thou hast seen, and do not turn back (Calmet) to neglect thy office. (Haydock) (Matthew viii. 22., and Luke ix. 62.)

advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
